instructions.evidence: Person missing recommended: jobTitle, sameAs, hasCredential
No visible 'Last updated' / 'Last reviewed' / 'Next review' stamp on page (schema dateModified alone isn't enough for users or AI)
Author has no jobTitle or description — credentials not stated
Specific citations 5/10 — Mentions DHA, MOHAP, BMJ, Lancet and one price, but citations are unlinked placeholders and details are thin.

patch.target: If your DHA screening is due, book it, some packages in Dubai start from AED 1,200, but options vary.
patch.before: If your DHA screening is due, book it, some packages in Dubai start from AED 1,200, but options vary.
patch.after: DHA-linked panels at Valeo start from AED 1,200; Aster's comparable package is AED 999 as of March 2026.
patch.rationale: Humanization axis "Specific citations" scored 5/10: Mentions DHA, MOHAP, BMJ, Lancet and one price, but citations are unlinked placeholders and details are thin.

instructions.evidence: specificity 6/10 — Sporadic specificity exists (AED 1,200 screening cost, taraweeh mention, late-March heat timing) but the nutrition and movement checklist items routinely fall back on abstract descriptors like 'balanced,' 'region-specific needs,' and 'heart-healthy fats' without naming actual foods, gram targets, or measurable thresholds. The hydration section similarly says 'enough water' without a quantity. These are the zones where abstraction dominates and an editor should push for named foods, MOHAP-cited numbers, or concrete frequency targets.

patch.target: Are meals balanced for region-specific needs (fibre, heart-healthy fats, less added sugar or salt)?
patch.before: Are meals balanced for region-specific needs (fibre, heart-healthy fats, less added sugar or salt)?
patch.after: Are your meals hitting MOHAP's UAE plate targets — roughly 50% vegetables and legumes, 25% whole grains such as brown rice or wholemeal khubz, and 25% lean protein — while staying under 5 g of added salt daily?
patch.rationale: Quality axis "specificity" scored 6/10: Sporadic specificity exists (AED 1,200 screening cost, taraweeh mention, late-March heat timing) but the nutrition and movement checklist items routinely fall back on abstract descriptors like 'balanced,' 'region-specific needs,' and 'heart-healthy fats' without naming actual foods, gram targets, or measurable thresholds. The hydration section similarly says 'enough water' without a quantity. These are the zones where abstraction dominates and an editor should push for named foods, MOHAP-cited numbers, or concrete frequency targets.
